summary: Russian forces reported control of Ivolzhanskoye in Sumy region and have conducted multiple drone strikes on civilian infrastructure in Sumy and Kharkiv. Ukrainian sources report Russian UAV attacks on a gas station near Sumy and a cinema in Konotop, causing civilian injuries. Both sides continue intensive UAV operations across multiple fronts.
key_points: Russian troops have liberated the village of Ivolzhanskoye in the Sumy region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31132).
Ukrainian officials say a Russian drone struck a gas station on the outskirts of Sumy, injuring three civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/82145).
A Russian drone attack on a cinema in Konotop, Sumy region, caused multiple civilian injuries including a leg and neck wound (Source: @UkraineNow/82146).
Ukrainian reports indicate Russian "Geranium" drones hit railway trains carrying military supplies in the Kharkiv region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31135).
TASS reports Russia has established control over Ivolzhansk in Sumy region (Source: @tass_agency/382236).
Ukrainian intelligence notes a large number of strike and reconnaissance UAVs operating over the Zaporozhye front, with continuous strikes on Stepnogorsk and Prymorsk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Belarusian relay stations used for Russian fire correction have been non‑operational since 22 June (Source: @UkraineNow/82151).
Russian UAV groups were observed moving from Kursk to Sumy and from Dnipropetrovsk toward northern Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/66243; @kpszsu/66200).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces have secured Ivolzhanskoye in Sumy and intensified UAV attacks on Ukrainian civilian targets, including a gas station near Sumy and a cinema in Konotop. Ukrainian reports detail ongoing small‑arms battles across Sumy, Kharkiv and the Zaporozhye front, with heavy UAV activity from both sides. Belarusian relay stations that aided Russian fire correction have been offline since 22 June, affecting Russian targeting capabilities.

points: Ukrainian authorities reported a Russian drone strike on a gas station on the outskirts of Sumy, injuring three civilians who are receiving medical care (Source: @UkraineNow/82145).
A Russian drone hit a cinema in Konotop, Sumy region, causing at least one severe leg injury and a neck wound to another civilian, according to the city mayor (Source: @UkraineNow/82146).
Ukrainian forces continue small‑arms engagements in the Sumy direction, notably in Bachevsk (Shostka district), Ivolzhansk, Pysarevka and Novaya Sech (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).

In the Kharkiv direction, Ukrainian units are engaged in the settlement of Kazachya Lopan and surrounding forest areas, as well as in Vovchansk sector villages Losevka and Ukrainskoe (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Ukrainian 291st Motorized Rifle Regiment is operating in the Zaporozhye area, conducting operations against Ukrainian snipers (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31119).
Russian Ministry of Defense announced the liberation of Ivolzhanskoye village in Sumy region, confirming control over the settlement (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31132).
TASS reported that Russian forces have established control over Ivolzhansk in Sumy region (Source: @tass_agency/382236).
Russian "Geranium" drones struck railway trains transporting Ukrainian military supplies in the Kharkiv region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31135).
Russian UAV activity includes groups moving from Kursk to Sumy and from Dnipropetrovsk toward northern Zaporizhzhia, indicating coordinated strike planning (Source: @kpszsu/66243; @kpszsu/66200).
Russian forces reported small‑arms battles in the Pavlovgrad district and conducted strikes on Sumy, Kharkiv and other locations overnight (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).

Ukrainian reports detail ongoing small‑arms fighting in Sumy region villages Ivolzhansk, Pysarevka, Novaya Sech and Bachevsk, with Russian forces holding Ivolzhansk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127; @ukr_leaks_eng/31132).
In Kharkiv region, Russian "Geranium" drones hit railway trains, and Ukrainian units are engaged in Kazachya Lopan and Vovchansk sector villages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31135; @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Ukrainian UAV groups are operating over the Zaporozhye front, striking Stepnogorsk and Prymorsk, while Russian UAVs conduct reconnaissance over the same area (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Four UAV groups were observed heading north in Sumy towards Shostka, indicating concentrated aerial activity on the eastern axis (Source: @eRadarrua/98011).
Russian small‑arms engagements were reported in the Pavlovgrad district, adding to the eastern front's intensity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).

Ukrainian intelligence noted a large number of strike and reconnaissance UAVs over the Zaporozhye front, with continuous operations suggesting heightened aerial activity in the north‑south corridor (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Ukrainian reports mention UAV groups moving from Kursk toward Sumy, indicating a northward UAV thrust (Source: @kpszsu/66243).
Four UAV groups were reported heading north in Sumy towards Shostka, reinforcing northern pressure (Source: @eRadarrua/98011).
Russian forces reported small‑arms battles in the Shostka district, specifically in Bachevsk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Ukrainian side reported a UAV strike on a gas station near Sumy, reflecting northern civilian target vulnerability (Source: @UkraineNow/82145).
Ukrainian forces are engaged in the Zaporozhye area, with the 291st Motorized Rifle Regiment operating there (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31119).
Russian UAVs have been observed moving from Dnipropetrovsk toward northern Zaporizhzhia, indicating southern‑to‑northern strike vectors (Source: @kpszsu/66200).
Ukrainian reports cite continuous UAV strikes on Stepnogorsk and Prymorsk in the Zaporozhye region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Russian "Geranium" drones targeted railway logistics in Kharkiv, affecting southern supply lines (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31135).
Sevastopol lost power after overloaded UAV operations, showing southern infrastructure impact (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Russian UAV groups are operating from Kursk toward Sumy and from Dnipropetrovsk toward Zaporizhzhia, indicating coordinated strike capabilities (Source: @kpszsu/66243; @kpszsu/66200).
Ukrainian intelligence reports a high density of strike and reconnaissance UAVs over the Zaporozhye front, with continuous engagements (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Four UAV groups were observed heading north in Sumy towards Shostka, suggesting concentrated aerial pressure (Source: @eRadarrua/98011).
Russian "Geranium" drones struck railway trains in Kharkiv, demonstrating precision strike capability against logistics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31135).
Sevastopol experienced a power outage after Russian UAV overloads, indicating the impact of UAV saturation on critical infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).

key_developments: Russian forces have secured Ivolzhanskoye in Sumy region, confirming territorial gain (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31132).
Ukrainian authorities report a Russian drone strike on a Sumy gas station, injuring three civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/82145).
A Russian drone attack on a cinema in Konotop caused multiple civilian injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/82146).
Russian "Geranium" drones hit railway trains carrying Ukrainian military supplies in Kharkiv (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31135).
Ukrainian small‑arms battles continue in Sumy villages Ivolzhansk, Pysarevka, Novaya Sech and Bachevsk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
High‑density UAV activity observed from Kursk to Sumy and Dnipropetrovsk to Zaporizhzhia, indicating coordinated aerial operations (Source: @kpszsu/66243; @kpszsu/66200).
Belarusian relay stations used for Russian fire correction have been offline since 22 June, potentially degrading Russian targeting (Source: @UkraineNow/82151).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian control of Ivolzhanskoye expands their foothold in Sumy, enabling further pressure on Ukrainian defensive lines. Intensified UAV strikes on civilian infrastructure in Sumy and Kharkiv demonstrate Russia's focus on disrupting logistics and morale. The cessation of Belarusian relay stations may limit Russian artillery accuracy, but continued UAV saturation poses a significant threat to Ukrainian command and control.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian forces are reinforcing small‑arms defenses in contested Sumy villages and increasing UAV counter‑measures in the Zaporozhye sector. International partners are monitoring the impact of Belarusian relay shutdowns on Russian targeting capabilities. Humanitarian agencies are responding to civilian casualties from drone attacks in Sumy and Konotop.
sections.intelligence_notes: UAV movement patterns suggest coordinated strike corridors from Kursk to Sumy and from Dnipropetrovsk to Zaporizhzhia, warranting heightened air defense alerts. The loss of power in Sevastopol highlights vulnerability of critical infrastructure to UAV overload tactics. Continued monitoring of Belarusian relay status is essential to assess changes in Russian artillery effectiveness.
